name: LokiJS
description: LokiJS is an in-memory, document-oriented JavaScript database with a MongoDB-like API. It stores collections of plain JavaScript objects, with built-in DynamicView for persistent query result sets, and supports optional persistence adapters for saving data to IndexedDB, WebSQL, localStorage, or a local JSON file. LokiJS runs entirely in-process in Node.js and browsers with zero external dependencies, making it ideal for mobile apps, Electron apps, and single-page applications that need fast local document storage.
format_on_disk: In-Memory with optional persistence via JSON file, IndexedDB, WebSQL, or localStorage adapters
core_strengths: MongoDB-like API for document queries with simple find, insert, update, remove
DynamicView for persistent live query result sets with sorted indexes
Zero dependencies with single-file embeddable builds for Node.js and browsers
Optional persistence adapters (IndexedDB, WebSQL, localStorage, JSON file)
Built-in indexing with compound indexes for fast document lookups
Small footprint suitable for mobile apps, Electron apps, and SPAs
best_use_cases: Client-side document storage for single-page applications and PWAs
Embedded database for Electron and Node.js desktop applications
Mobile app local storage via browser adapters (IndexedDB, WebSQL)
Prototyping and rapid development with a MongoDB-like query pattern
Offline-first apps using in-memory storage with periodic persistence saves
remarks: Project was abandoned in 2022 and is no longer maintained. The official website redirects to RxDB, which has become the spiritual successor in the local-first JavaScript database space. LokiJS loads the entire database into memory, making it unsuitable for datasets larger than available RAM. SyncProxy for real-time server sync was a planned feature but never reached production readiness. Use RxDB for new projects; LokiJS is included for reference and legacy compatibility.
